text:
Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
Customers upgrading to Enterprise OS 2026.1 should be aware that Driver Pack versions prior to 12.4.0 were not validated against this release and may exhibit reduced peripheral stability, particularly with USB-C docking accessories and external display adapters. Fleets that have not yet standardized on the current Driver Pack baseline are encouraged to complete that upgrade ahead of any OS migration work.

Rule Type:
Min Version Constraint

Subject:
Enterprise OS 2026.1

Predicate:
REQUIRES

Object:
Driver Pack 12.4.0

Relationship:
Enterprise OS 2026.1 REQUIRES Driver Pack 12.4.0.

Requirements:
Driver Pack must be at least 12.4.0.

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: Driver Pack must be at least 12.4.0.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Upgrade Driver Pack to version 12.4.0 or later before migrating to Enterprise OS 2026.1 to ensure peripheral stability.

Compliance Impact:
A system that matches the conditions but does not meet the requirements is non-compliant. Impact severity: Warning.

Evidence:
Customers upgrading to Enterprise OS 2026.1 should be aware that Driver Pack versions prior to 12.4.0 were not validated against this release and may exhibit reduced peripheral stability, particularly with USB-C docking accessories and external display adapters. Fleets that have not yet standardized on the current Driver Pack baseline are encouraged to complete that upgrade ahead of any OS migration work. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000377, page 1.

Confidence:
0.9. The text explicitly states that versions prior to 12.4.0 were not validated and may exhibit stability issues with the specified OS release.

Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
Separately, field reports indicate that ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series units shipped with System Firmware versions in the 8.1.x line may report inaccurate battery health percentages following extended sleep cycles. While this does not affect charging safety, IT administrators managing large laptop fleets may wish to prioritize the firmware refresh for these device classes during the v6.4.2 maintenance cycle rather than treating it as a routine, lower-priority update.

Rule Type:
Known Issue Fixed

Subject:
ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series and System Firmware 8.1.x

Predicate:
REQUIRES_FIX_IN

Object:
System Firmware v6.4.2

Relationship:
ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series and System Firmware 8.1.x REQUIRES_FIX_IN System Firmware v6.4.2.

Requirements:
System Firmware must be at least v6.4.2.

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: System Firmware must be at least v6.4.2.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Update System Firmware to version v6.4.2 or later for ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series units on firmware 8.1.x.

Compliance Impact:
A system that matches the conditions but does not meet the requirements is non-compliant. Impact severity: Info.

Evidence:
Separately, field reports indicate that ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series units shipped with System Firmware versions in the 8.1.x line may report inaccurate battery health percentages following extended sleep cycles. While this does not affect charging safety, IT administrators managing large laptop fleets may wish to prioritize the firmware refresh for these device classes during the v6.4.2 maintenance cycle rather than treating it as a routine, lower-priority update. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000378, page 1.

Confidence:
0.7. The text suggests prioritizing a firmware refresh to v6.4.2 to resolve a known issue with battery health reporting in versions 8.1.x

metadata.subject: ProBook and Enterprise Laptop Series and System Firmware 8.1.x

text:
Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
It has also been observed that Security Agent deployments paired with Enterprise OS builds older than 2025.2 can produce inconsistent telemetry reporting to centralized management consoles, even when all other components meet published minimums. Affected environments should treat this as a signal to revisit their OS upgrade timeline rather than assuming the Security Agent installation itself is at fault.

Rule Type:
Known Issue Fixed

Subject:
Security Agent and Enterprise OS 2025.2

Predicate:
REQUIRES_FIX_IN

Object:
Enterprise OS 2025.2

Relationship:
Security Agent and Enterprise OS 2025.2 REQUIRES_FIX_IN Enterprise OS 2025.2.

Requirements:
Enterprise OS must be at least 2025.2.

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: Enterprise OS must be at least 2025.2.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Upgrade Enterprise OS to version 2025.2 or newer to ensure consistent telemetry reporting.

Compliance Impact:
A system that matches the conditions but does not meet the requirements is non-compliant. Impact severity: Warning.

Evidence:
It has also been observed that Security Agent deployments paired with Enterprise OS builds older than 2025.2 can produce inconsistent telemetry reporting to centralized management consoles, even when all other components meet published minimums. Affected environments should treat this as a signal to revisit their OS upgrade timeline rather than assuming the Security Agent installation itself is at fault. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000379, page 1.

Confidence:
0.9. The text explicitly states that builds older than 2025.2 produce inconsistent telemetry reporting.

Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
EdgeStation Workstations require NIC Firmware 4.2.0 or later when deployed with Platform Driver Pack 12.5.0. This requirement does not apply to ProBook Series, Enterprise Laptop Series, or ComputeNode Servers, which do not ship with the same onboard NIC controller and are exempt from this requirement.

Rule Type:
Min Version Constraint

Subject:

Predicate:

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: NIC Firmware must be at least 4.2.0.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Update NIC Firmware to 4.2.0 or later when using Platform Driver Pack 12.5.0 on EdgeStation Workstations.

Compliance Impact:
A system that matches the conditions but does not meet the requirements is non-compliant. Impact severity: Critical.

Evidence:
EdgeStation Workstations require NIC Firmware 4.2.0 or later when deployed with Platform Driver Pack 12.5.0. This requirement does not apply to ProBook Series, Enterprise Laptop Series, or ComputeNode Servers, which do not ship with the same onboard NIC controller and are exempt from this requirement. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000390, page 1.

Confidence:
1.0. Explicit requirement stated in the text with clear version constraints and listed exceptions.

Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000394, page 1.

Confidence:
0.6. The text describes a tolerated state (deferring an upgrade) rather than a strict requirement, implying a general requirement for version 12.5.0 that is currently being waived for specific fleets.

Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
Per REC-003, the driver pack must be current before any Enterprise OS migration is attempted.

Rule Type:
Readiness Requirement

Subject:
Enterprise OS Enterprise OS migration

Predicate:
DEPENDS_ON

Object:
driver pack current

Relationship:
Enterprise OS Enterprise OS migration DEPENDS_ON driver pack current.

Requirements:
driver pack must be exactly current.

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: driver pack must be exactly current.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Ensure the driver pack is updated to the current version before attempting Enterprise OS migration.

Compliance Impact:
A system that matches the conditions but does not meet the requirements is non-compliant. Impact severity: Critical.

Evidence:
Per REC-003, the driver pack must be current before any Enterprise OS migration is attempted. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000430, page 1.

Confidence:
0.9. Explicitly states the driver pack must be current before OS migration.

Compatibility Rule

Rule Description:
Devices that skip Step 1 and attempt the BIOS upgrade directly against Firmware 7.9.0 may encounter installation failures or the boot-delay condition described in KI-001.

Rule Type:
Update Order Constraint

Subject:
firmware 7.9.0

Predicate:
MUST_PRECEDE

Object:
Step 1

Relationship:
firmware 7.9.0 MUST_PRECEDE Step 1.

Requirements:
Step 1 must be installed Step 1.

Dependencies:
The compliant state depends on: Step 1 must be installed Step 1.

Conflicts:
No conflicts documented.

Exceptions:
No exceptions documented.

Remediation:
Perform Step 1 before attempting the BIOS upgrade if currently on Firmware 7.9.0.

Compliance Impact:
Attempting the dependent update before its prerequisite is satisfied is non-compliant. Impact severity: Critical.

Evidence:
Devices that skip Step 1 and attempt the BIOS upgrade directly against Firmware 7.9.0 may encounter installation failures or the boot-delay condition described in KI-001. Source document DOC-CA114A84AE60, chunk CHUNK-000434, page 1.

Confidence:
0.9. The text explicitly warns that skipping Step 1 before upgrading from Firmware 7.9.0 may lead to installation failures or boot delays.
